fantasy: You awaken in a stone chamber, torchlight guttering in a draft you can't place.
The dragon's shadow slides over the wheat field, and every farmhand drops flat but you.
The queen's summons arrived sewn into the lining of your coat. You only found it now.
At the crossroads shrine the offered coins are gone, and something left three wet footprints.
scifi: The nav computer beeps: jump complete. The star outside is the wrong colour.
You crack the airlock and the derelict's corridor is warm, though its reactor died a century ago.
Your new prosthetic arm just typed a message you didn't write.
The colony's water ration halved overnight, and the council insists nothing changed.
horror: The neighbours' dog has barked at your basement door for two hours. You don't own a dog.
Every photograph in the house now shows one more person standing at the back.
The tenant upstairs died Tuesday. You still hear their kettle click off each morning.
You find your own name in the guest book of a hotel you've never visited, dated next week.
mystery: The victim locked the door from the inside, and the only key is in your pocket.
Every clock in the manor stopped at 3:14 — except the study's, running ten minutes fast.
The witness describes the thief perfectly. The thief is you, and you were asleep.
The dead man's last ledger entry is a single word, in your handwriting.
romance: They returned your umbrella a week later, dry, with a note you can't stop rereading.
You've shared this platform every morning for a year, and today they spoke first.
The wedding you're catering is your ex's — and so, apparently, is the best man's toast.
The bookshop clerk keeps slipping the wrong receipts into your bag, with phone numbers on them.
literary: Your father's house sold today. You kept only the key, which fits nothing you own.
The letter is thirty years late, addressed to a version of you that no longer exists.
You return to the coast town every summer to not talk about the same thing.
At the reunion, everyone remembers a you that you're fairly sure never happened.
comedy: You accepted the job before realising 'Assistant to the Wizard' involved actual wizards, and paperwork.
The self-checkout has flagged your single banana as a 'suspicious item' for the fourth time.
Your roommate replaced all the furniture with slightly smaller furniture. No one else notices.
You've been promoted to 'Head of the Committee to Reduce Committees.'
